description: Amyema cambagei, commonly known as sheoak mistletoe, is a species of flowering plant, an epiphytic hemiparasitic plant of the family Loranthaceae endemic to Australia, and found in New South Wales and Queensland in sclerophyll forest and woodland on several species of Casuarinaceae.
origin: NSW, NSW CT, NSW NS, AUSTRALIA, QLD QU

care.tips: Native to: NSW, NSW CT, NSW NS, AUSTRALIA, QLD QU

description: Amyema congener, commonly known as the variable mistletoe, is a species of flowering plant, an epiphytic hemiparasitic plant of the family Loranthaceae from eastern Australia. It is found on members of the genera Allocasuarina, Acacia and some exotic species.
origin: NSW, NSW CT, NSW NS, AUSTRALIA, QLD QU

care.tips: Native to: NSW NS, AUSTRALIA, NSW, QLD, QLD CS

description: Amyema maidenii is a species of flowering plant within the genus Amyema, an epiphytic hemiparasitic plant of the family Loranthaceae native to Australia and found Australia-wide in the inland.
origin: NSW, NSW CT, NSW NS, QLD QU, NTA OO

care.tips: Native to: AUSTRALIA, WAU WA, NTA OO, SOA OO, WAU

description: Amyema miquelii, also known as box mistletoe, is a species of flowering plant, an epiphytic hemiparasitic plant of the family Loranthaceae, found attached to several species of Australian eucalypt and occasionally on some species of Acacia. It is the most widespread of the Australian Mistletoes, occurring mainly to the west of the Great Dividing Range. It has shiny leaves and red flowers arranged in groups of 3.
origin: NSW, NSW CT, NSW NS, QLD QU, NTA OO
synonyms: ["Loranthus miquelii var. minor", "Loranthus pendulus var. miquelii", "Loranthus miquelii var. micranthus", "Loranthus miquelii", "Loranthus aurantiacus", "Amyema aurantiaca", "Dendrophthoe miquelii"]

care.tips: Native to: AUSTRALIA, WAU WA, NTA OO, SOA OO, VIC

description: Amyema miraculosa, also known as the fleshy mistletoe and the round-leaf mistletoe, is an Australian native mistletoe found in all states except Tasmania. It is a woody, hemiparasitic plant, in the Loranthaceae family. Being hemiparasitic, it draws water and minerals from its host, however it photosynthesises to manufacture its own supply of carbohydrates.

Amyema pendula, also known as drooping mistletoe or furry drooping mistletoe, is a species of flowering plant, an epiphytic hemiparasitic plant of the family Loranthaceae, found attached to several species of Australian eucalypt and occasionally on some species of Acacia. It is endemic to south-eastern Australia. It is the most common mistletoe in Victoria, especially on the coastal side of the Great Dividing Range. It has shiny leaves and red flowers arranged in groups of 3 or 4.
origin: SOA OO, NSW, NSW CT, NSW NS, AUSTRALIA

care.tips: Native to: AUSTRALIA, NSW NS, NSW, SOA OO, QLD

description: Amyema preissii, commonly known as wireleaf mistletoe, is a species of mistletoe, an epiphytic, hemiparasitic plant of the family Loranthaceae. It is native to Australia where it has been recorded from all mainland states. The flowers are red and up to 26 mm long. The fruits are white or pink, globose and 8–10 mm in diameter. Its habitat is sclerophyll forest and woodland where it is often found on wattles.
origin: NSW, NSW CT, NSW NS, QLD QU, NTA OO
synonyms: ["Loranthus preissii var. didymus", "Amyema scoparia", "Loranthus preissii", "Loranthus scoparius", "Loranthus linifolius"]

care.tips: Native to: AUSTRALIA, WAU WA, NTA OO, SOA OO, VIC

description: Amyema quandang is a species of hemi-parasitic shrub which is widespread throughout the mainland of Australia, especially arid inland regions, sometimes referred to as the grey mistletoe.
origin: NSW, NSW CT, NSW NS, QLD QU, VIC OO
synonyms: ["Amyema pruinosa", "Amyema quandang var. bancroftii", "Amyema nutans", "Loranthus quandang var. typicus", "Loranthus canus", "Loranthus cunninghamii", "Amyema cana", "Loranthus quandang", "Loranthus pruinosus", "Dendrophthoe pruinosa"]

care.tips: Native to: NSW, NSW CT, NSW NS, QLD QU, VIC OO

description: Amylotheca dictyophleba is a member of the mistletoe family, Loranthaceae, and was first described in 1860 by Ferdinand von Mueller as Loranthus dictyophlebus, but in 1894, Philippe Édouard Léon Van Tieghem transferred it to his newly described genus, Amylotheca.
origin: NSW, NSW CT, NSW NS, VAN, PACIFIC
synonyms: ["Elytranthe tenuifolia", "Elytranthe lifuensis", "Amylotheca pyramidata", "Amylotheca comptonii", "Amyema oligantha", "Elytranthe pyramidata", "Amylotheca tenuifolia", "Loranthus beauverdianus", "Amyema francii", "Loranthus dictyophlebus"]

care.tips: Native to: AUSTRALIA, NWC OO, ASIA TROPICAL, PAPUASIA, NWC

description: Gaiadendron is a genus of parasitic shrubs or trees in the family Loranthaceae. It solely comprises the species Gaiadendron punctatum, which is found in North and South America.
origin: PAN OO, NIC, BOL, BZN AM, BRAZIL
synonyms: ["Gaiadendron ellipticum", "Gaiadendron lanceolatum", "Loranthus laurifolius", "Gaiadendron macranthum", "Taguaria vera", "Struthanthus punctatus", "Gaiadendron puracense", "Gaiadendron poasense", "Taguaria punctata", "Taguaria puracensis"]

description: Ileostylus micranthus, commonly known as green mistletoe pikirangi; pirinoa; pirirangi; pirita; small-flowered mistletoe, is a species of mistletoe in the family Loranthaceae.
origin: NFK NI, AUSTRALIA, NZN OO, NZS OO, NFK LH

description: Lysiana exocarpi, commonly known as harlequin mistletoe, is a species of hemiparasitic shrub, endemic to Australia. It is in the Gondwanan family Loranthaceae and is probably the most derived genus of that family with 12 pairs of chromosomes. The Loranthaceae is the most diverse family in the mistletoe group with over 900 species worldwide and including the best known species in Australia. Mistletoes are notable for their relationships with other species.
origin: SOA, AUSTRALASIA, NSW, NSW CT, VIC OO

description: Lysiana subfalcata, common name Northern mistletoe, is a spreading to pendulous hemi-parasitic shrub in the Loranthaceae which occurs in all mainland states of Australia except Victoria.
Muellerina eucalyptoides, commonly known as creeping mistletoe, is a hemiparasitic aerial shrub in the family Loranthaceae. The species is endemic to Australia.

Nuytsia floribunda is a hemiparasitic tree found in Western Australia. The species is known locally as the Christmas tree or Western Australian Christmas tree. The display of intensely bright flowers during the austral summer coincides with the Christmas season.

Peraxilla tetrapetala, commonly known as red mistletoe, is a parasitic plant in the family Loranthaceae, endemic to New Zealand and found on both the North and South Islands. The Māori names for the plant are pikirangi, pirirangi and roeroe. It is a shrub up to one metre tall with glabrous leaves. The flowers are 4–5 mm long, bright red to orange in colour which split open to the base.

Psittacanthus calyculatus is a species of Neotropical mistletoe in the family Loranthaceae, native to Colombia, 
Mexico, the Mexican Gulf, and Venezuela.
Psittacanthus robustus is a species of Neotropical mistletoe in the family Loranthaceae, which is found in Brazil, Colombia, Guyana, and Venezuela.
